# Phase 10.0.20 Commit Hygiene Plan

Status: Planning-only. This plan does not create executable workflows, runtime authority, provider authority, infrastructure authority, deployment authority, autonomous governance, APIs, background jobs, autonomous merge systems, automated branch-authority systems, or execution authority.

## Objective

Preserve deterministic repository evolution, commit hygiene, branch governance, merge survivability, repository lineage, and long-horizon source-control continuity after repository-integrity doctrine.

## Repository Hygiene Posture

Current implementation posture: UNSTABLE.

Reason: unrelated runtime/application modifications, generated Prisma diffs, and many untracked files exist, but no staged changes were detected during pre-build inspection. Phase 10.0.20 must remain path-isolated to governance documentation and planning files.

## In-Scope Documents

- Constitutional commit-hygiene doctrine.
- Governance-aware branch-management philosophy.
- Deterministic repository-evolution framework.
- Merge-survivability governance standards.
- Repository-history integrity doctrine.
- Anti-branch-fragmentation framework.
- Constitutional source-control continuity architecture.
- Audit-safe merge philosophy.
- Repository lineage-preservation standards.
- Governance-aware commit-traceability framework.
- Phase 10.0.20 commit hygiene summary.

## Validation Checklist

- `git status` inspected.
- `git diff` inspected.
- `git branch` inspected.
- `git log --oneline --decorate -n 20` inspected.
- Staged changes inspected.
- Branch divergence inspected.
- Unrelated runtime/application changes reported.
- Mixed-scope worktree state classified.
- Governance/runtime boundary integrity preserved.
- No runtime activation exists.
- No execution authorization exists.
- No deployment authorization exists.
- No provider onboarding exists.
- No orchestration activation exists.
- No infrastructure activation exists.
- No autonomous merge system exists.
- No automated branch-authority system exists.

## Review Notes

- Commits are constitutional history surfaces.
- Branches are custody surfaces, not authority surfaces.
- Merges must preserve scope, evidence, boundary clarity, and rollback meaning.
- Dirty worktree ambiguity must be classified and reported.
- Do not include: runtime enablement, providers, adapters, deployment paths, credentials, OAuth, APIs, fetch/axios, Prisma/Supabase changes, background jobs, autonomous governance, autonomous merge systems, automated branch-authority systems, or execution systems.
